title: The Alhambra
topic_path: Art of the Islamic world 640 to now

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Alhambra was built by the [[☃ dropdown 1]] Dynasty.**



![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/02003bf637d6a15a5160df86ca9f6804.jpg)
hints:
**The Alhambra was built by the Nasrid Dynasty.**

**[Read this essay to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-1300-medieval---byzantine-eras/islamic-art/a/medieval-period-the-alhambra)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Gardens, water fountains, canals, and pools are a recurring theme in The Alhambra.**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/02003bf637d6a15a5160df86ca9f6804.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**The architects of The Alhambra used the sight, sound, and cooling effects of water to decorate indoor and outdoor spaces of the palaces.** 

**[Read this essay to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-1300-medieval---byzantine-eras/islamic-art/a/medieval-period-the-alhambra)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following was used to decorate the interior and exterior walls of the palaces?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
** The artists of The Alhambra used painted tile and carved (and sometimes painted) stucco to decorate the interior and exterior walls of the palaces.**

**[Read this essay to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-1300-medieval---byzantine-eras/islamic-art/a/medieval-period-the-alhambra)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Several grand rooms in The Alhambra have vaulted ceilings decorated with [[☃ dropdown 1]].**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5bc4f0856b8973cdebba932a894db2ce.jpg)
hints:
**Several grand rooms in The Alhambra have vaulted ceilings decoraed with muqarnas. Muqarnas are intricately carved brackets that hold up a vaulted ceiling.**

**[Read this essay to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-1300-medieval---byzantine-eras/islamic-art/a/medieval-period-the-alhambra)**
